description: Next weekend there is a patrol leaders' camp. We are going to the mountains and it will be great, but I also have a test on Monday and should study.

leftChoice.text: Go to camp

description: You have a test tomorrow and you have not prepared at all.

leftChoice.text: Study all night

rightChoice.text: Study until a fixed time and accept the result

description: Your favorite band is playing a concert this weekend.

leftChoice.text: Great! I'm going!

description: Your troop leader tells you to write the yearly plan, because the deadline is in 3 days.

leftChoice.text: Sit down and write it

rightChoice.text: One day later will not matter

description: You have a free afternoon. What do you do?

leftChoice.text: Study and catch up on scouting work

description: It is test and exam season.

leftChoice.text: Study all week without leaving home

rightChoice.text: Study, but keep some time to rest!

description: Your troop has a meeting on Saturday, and your parents suggested a family weekend trip.

leftChoice.text: Go and help at the troop meeting

description: Your friends invite you to study together.

leftChoice.text: Meet them, it will be good to see them and we will study too.

description: You caught a cold.

leftChoice.text: Go to school or university!

description: A new season of a great Netflix show just came out.

leftChoice.text: Watch all night

description: Your good friend's party is on Friday, and your troop meeting starts Saturday at 10.

leftChoice.text: Leave early and get more sleep

rightChoice.text: Party all night and arrive exhausted

description: During the summer, a scout training course overlaps with a trip with friends to the lakes.

leftChoice.text: Go with friends

description: Unfortunately, you took on too much and everything is starting to fall apart.

leftChoice.text: Uhhh

description: Final exams or university exams are coming, and you realize you have not studied regularly. Things are not looking good.

leftChoice.text: Oops

description: You and your leadership team had a great flow of ideas for scout activities!

leftChoice.text: Yes!

description: There is a very difficult scout in your troop.

leftChoice.text: Talk with them about their problems and try to solve the difficult situation

rightChoice.text: Leave it alone and hope it solves itself

description: You are planning a camp. You can make it an epic long-weekend trip, or use a normal weekend.

leftChoice.text: Use a normal weekend and save the long weekend for friends or rest

rightChoice.text: Use the long weekend. It will be an amazing expedition!

description: You are failing a school or university year, final exams are near, and your troop is falling apart.

leftChoice.text: Step away from scouting; school comes first

rightChoice.text: Keep working with the unit and study as much as you can

description: You are organizing a camp. You can choose a cheaper, less ambitious program or a more ambitious and expensive one.

leftChoice.text: Apply for funding and handle the paperwork

rightChoice.text: Choose the cheaper option; there is no time for finance paperwork

description: Your time is limited. You must choose between a private relationship and a scout meeting or fundraiser where the troop really needs support.

leftChoice.text: Skip the troop event and spend time on the relationship

rightChoice.text: Go to the meeting or fundraiser

description: Your time is limited. You must choose between staying a troop leader and taking a job.

leftChoice.text: Take the job and let the troop slip

rightChoice.text: Do not take the job, but keep the troop running perfectly!

description: Sports or extracurricular activities overlap with scout meetings. What do you do?

leftChoice.text: Choose sports or activities.

description: Many new scouts want to join your troop.

leftChoice.text: Accept all of them, even though the troop is already large and quality will drop

rightChoice.text: Accept scouts up to a clear limit and turn the rest away

description: Heavy rain flooded your storage room. To save the camp equipment, you must act immediately!

leftChoice.text: Gather the troop and dry everything

rightChoice.text: Ignore it; you do not have time now

description: You get sick right before an important leadership team trip.

leftChoice.text: Go despite being sick; you will not leave them

description: Your grades have dropped dramatically. You are at risk of failing.

leftChoice.text: Drop everything and study for retakes

description: Your girlfriend or boyfriend is tired of you never having time.

leftChoice.text: Choose to save the relationship

description: Conflict in the leadership team! Two people want to leave unless you resolve the dispute.

leftChoice.text: Organize mediation (it will take the whole weekend)

rightChoice.text: Let them leave; you will manage alone

description: You are completely exhausted and falling asleep in classes and meetings.

leftChoice.text: Take a month off from scouting

rightChoice.text: Drink another coffee and push on

description: Your scout room needs a major renovation, and the community is threatening to evict you.

leftChoice.text: Renovate it yourselves (hard work)
